task.title: Customizing build steps
task.shortdesc: You can customize the build procedures that are designed by your MVS system administrator by overriding the default values used in the steps.

taskbody.prereq: You must know the names of the steps within these build procedures and what values must be modified. Your MVS system administrator can provide you with this information.
taskbody.context: You must customize build steps before you attempt to build your COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler source code.

steps.stepsection: The following steps can be used to customize a build step for COBOL source code, but you can use a similar process to customize a build step for PL/I or Assembler source code.

step.cmd: Click the COBOL, PL/I, or Assembler tab of the property group editor.
For COBOL or PL/I, select the procedure you want to customize in the table and click Enable procedure.
step.info: The procedure must be enabled before you can customize it.

step.stepresult: The Add a New Step area opens.

step.cmd: Type the name of the step you want to customize in the Step Name field.

info.note: If no step with the specified name exists in the selected procedure, the build procedure returns a JCL error when submitted. You can add only that steps that exist in the procedure that is designed by your MVS system administrator.

step.cmd: Specify any options that must be passed to the step in the Options field.
Specify any additional JCL in the Additional JCL field.
step.stepxmp: For example, you might use this field to override any default values defined in the step by your MVS system administrator.

taskbody.result: The customized build step is added under the selected procedure in the table.
taskbody.postreq: You can now modify the step definition by double-clicking it. You can also change the order in which steps are run by selecting a step and clicking Up and Down.
